title: Dactylorhiza × thellungiana
intro: Dactylorhiza × thellungiana, also known as Thellungiella, is a hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza iberica and Dactylorhiza saccifera. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza × thellungiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects. Dactylorhiza × thellungiana is commonly used in herbal medicine, with preparations including dried roots and extracts, as well as teas, capsules, and tinctures, often used to support cardiovascular health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × thellungiana is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza hatagirea and Dactylorhiza purpurella. This plant is classified under the family Orchidaceae and the genus Dactylorhiza. It is a unique and rare species found in specific regions. The classification of Dactylorhiza × thellungiana helps in understanding its characteristics and behavior. This classification also aids in the identification and conservation of the species.
uses: Dactylorhiza × thellungiana, also known as Thluggen's Spotted Orchid, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. The plant's extracts have been reported to exhibit potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. In traditional medicine, Dactylorhiza × thellungiana has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's roots and rhizomes have been used to make infusions and tinctures, which are believed to aid in digestion and relieve symptoms of fever. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Dactylorhiza × thellungiana for medicinal purposes.
